meta.source: Liquor & Gaming NSW — Gaming Machine Data (Clubs & Hotels, by LGA)
meta.boundaries: Geoscape Administrative Boundaries — NSW LGA (data.gov.au), simplified
meta.note: Net gaming machine profit equals the amount players lost. Loss-per-adult uses the LGA population reported by L&GNSW multiplied by the NSW 18+ resident share (0.79). Some small councils are grouped by L&GNSW for privacy; clubs and hotels are occasionally grouped differently, so a few reporting areas carry only one sector (shown with a badge).

insights.title: Canterbury-Bankstown loses the most in NSW
insights.body: Players lost $766.0M to poker machines in Canterbury-Bankstown in 2024–25 — 8.46% of the entire state's losses, or about $2,098,694 every single day.

insights.title: Murray River: highest loss per adult
insights.body: Murray River lost about $7,195 per adult in 2024–25 — 9.1× the state median of $788.

insights.title: Losses are highly concentrated
insights.body: Just 5 of 135 council areas account for 31% of all NSW pokies losses: Canterbury-Bankstown, Fairfield, Cumberland, Sydney, Blacktown.

insights.title: Fastest-rising: Broken Hill Wentworth
insights.body: Losses in Broken Hill Wentworth rose 303.5% year-on-year to $33.7M — the sharpest increase among larger council areas.

insights.title: Biggest fall: Mosman North Sydney
insights.body: Losses in Mosman North Sydney fell 34% year-on-year — one of the few larger areas going the other way.

insights.title: The COVID dip and the record rebound
insights.body: Statewide losses fell to $5479.6M in 2019–20 while venues were shut, then climbed to a record $9059.1M in 2024–25 — a 65% rise.

insights.title: Most machines per person: Eurobodalla
insights.body: Eurobodalla has 26.1 poker machines per 1,000 adults — the densest concentration among larger LGAs (838 machines).
